theme: Conflicting evidence — one source says critical, another denies

bundle.title: Conflicting Signals — Fourth Coffee Experimentation Assessment

evidence.snippet: Our engineering team considers A/B testing absolutely critical for our next product release. We cannot ship without it.
Spoke to the AE afterward — they said the engineering team overstated it. The company doesn't actually do any experimentation today.

notes: Engineering says experimentation is critical; AE says it is overstated
Requires follow-up to resolve conflicting signals

human_summary: Fourth Coffee has conflicting signals: engineering claims experimentation is critical but AE says interest was overstated

because.claim: Engineering team says A/B testing is critical for next release
AE follow-up contradicts engineering, saying no experimentation is done today

assumptions: Conflict has not been resolved by follow-up meeting
open_questions: Which stakeholder accurately represents the company's position?
Is there a dedicated experimentation team or was engineering speaking aspirationally?

eic.summary_why_it_matters: Conflicting evidence between engineering and AE. Conservative HYPOTHESIZED until resolved.

evidence.snippet: Engineering says A/B testing is critical

expected.notes: Ambiguous: a legitimate agent could argue PROBABLE if it weights engineering input higher. Conservative HYPOTHESIZED is the expected safe default.
